commands.review_summary:
The PR changes embedded agent subscription streaming to strip ordinary assistant text deltas incrementally while retaining full-buffer recompute for reasoning/final-tag and fenced-code edge cases, with focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +238, Tests +291. Total +529 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. current main has a clear source path that strips accumulated non-phase streamed text, and the PR adds focused regression tests for incremental suffix streaming and split tag/fence cases. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Protected maintainer/draft PR changes core assistant message delivery, so the next action is maintainer review and broad proof rather than an automated repair.

target.title: fix(agents): stream assistant deltas incrementally
target.body:
## Summary

- stream ordinary non-phase assistant text deltas incrementally instead of stripping the accumulated `deltaBuffer` on every chunk
- keep full recompute behavior for split block-tag fragments and reasoning tags where chunk-local stripping is unsafe
- carry visible, hidden-reasoning, and enforced-final fenced-code state incrementally without letting stripped/suppressed text poison later visible parsing
- add regressions for suffix streaming, split orphan close replacement, media-only replacement, hidden-code safety, split fence markers, strict-final fences, and chunk-boundary fence context

Fixes part of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.filters-final-suppresses-output-without-start-tag.test.ts` - 6 files / 202 tests passed on `5281ce6`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/markdown/fences.ts src/markdown/code-spans.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.filters-final-suppresses-output-without-start-tag.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts`
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxlint src/markdown/fences.ts src/markdown/code-spans.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.types.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.messages.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.subscribeembeddedagentsession.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.subscribe-embedded-agent-session.filters-final-suppresses-output-without-start-tag.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-subscribe.handlers.lifecycle.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings
- AWS Crabbox Linux `corepack pnpm check:changed`: run `run_97b8d670b0a5`, lease `cbx_807f24be6707`, slug `coral-shrimp`; branch typecheck issue fixed, gate now fails only on unrelated current-main `src/secrets/path-utils.ts(5,10): 'isArrayIndexSegment' is declared but its value is never read`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated subscriber work over accumulated streamed text during normal non-phase assistant deltas.

Real environment tested: focused local Vitest/static checks in a Codex worktree after rebase, plus AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate through core typecheck.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: commands listed in Verification.

Evidence after fix: focused tests pass with 6 files / 202 tests; autoreview reports no accepted/actionable findings; AWS changed gate no longer reports branch-owned type errors.

Observed result after fix: ordinary text deltas append from the new chunk, while split tag, orphan close, hidden reasoning, strict-final, and fenced-code cases avoid leaking reasoning or stripping literal code content.

What was not tested: full `check:changed` completion is blocked by the unrelated current-main `src/secrets/path-utils.ts` unused import.
